title: Domain App Grant Config v1
description: The workspace `config/grants.json` contract (EPIC-CORE-012, OQ #29/#31). Declares capability grants for DOMAIN APPS ONLY: `app` must match `domain-app:<name>` — first-party identities (`cli`, `sidecar:*`, `skill:*`) are unrepresentable here and their in-code registry can never be overridden by config. The grantable scope set structurally excludes `ALWAYS_DENIED_SCOPES` (`write:raw`, `delete:*`, `read:secure_refs`): no schema-valid document can name them, and the runtime resolver hard-denies them anyway (defense in depth). Loading is FAIL-CLOSED whole-file (OQ #31): any invalid content — including DUPLICATE `app` entries, which JSON Schema cannot express and the loader rejects additionally — rejects the entire file as `grant_config_invalid` and no external grants load. A missing config file is valid and means no external grants (default-deny).

version.description: Config version. Breaking changes bump this.

grants.description: One entry per domain app. Duplicate `app` values are additionally rejected by the loader (fail closed — never merged, never last-write-wins).

domainAppId.description: Domain-app caller identity. Reserved first-party identities (cli, sidecar:*, skill:*) do not match this pattern by construction.

grantableScope.description: Exactly the operational scopes a domain app may be granted or denied. `write:raw`, `delete:*`, and `read:secure_refs` are deliberately absent and cannot be expressed.

allow.description: Scopes granted to the app (default-deny: anything not listed is denied). An empty list is valid and grants nothing.

deny.description: Optional explicit denies; deny overrides allow in the resolver.
